rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/6/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/21/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET BOYD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The proof of service was verified on 4/30/21, but the notice was mailed on 5/1/2020 [sic] (or not until 5/1/2021?). What are the co-executors proposing to distribute at this time? Distribution is NOT shown in the petition. If they are seeking to distribute the entire estate but for the $20,000 cash reserve, then this is not a preliminary distribution, and all of the securities must also be clearly set forth in the petition (prayer) and in the proposed order. [CRC 7.651(b).] (The value of the securities does not need to be in the proposed distribution or order, as the value changes; only the number of shares is necessary.) The final paragraph of part 6 of the petition suggests that the decedent owned real property. None was inventoried and appraised in the estate. The sum in paragraph 16 of the petition does not agree with the attached schedule without number or exhibit letter. It appears, however, that the difference is the filing fee for this petition, which could be paid out of the reserve or requested in the petition for final distribution. The schedule of receipts (bank interest) is not, in fact, detailed. [CRC 7.550(b)(6).] Schedule F (?) showing Securities at Appraised Value does not agree with the Probate Referee's total shown on Attachment 2 of the I&A. (It is immaterial who the decedent's heirs-at-law are at this point, as she died testate.) Courtesy copies of papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/2/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FAMILY TRUST UNDER WILL OF HERBERT D. LEWIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: It is unclear how net income is calculated. It appears that the trustee just distributes all income to herself. How did she arrive at an additional $471.15 due to her? Why is there a cash carryover of $88.26 from the first (actually 10th) account? The Summary of Account does not show separate income and principal receipts. Why is the $1,500 the beneficiary reimbursed to the trust shown as a separate asset when it was reflected as a receipt in the 10th account? Schedule B (which should be just gains on sale or other disposition) includes capital gains and reinvested dividend, which should be reflected on the informational schedule only (or the receipts schedule as principal). The trusteeâs handwritten time sheets do not fully comply with CRC 7.776 or LRSF 14.53. Courtesy copies of papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/9/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required for the fees requested. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Courtesy copies and the proposed order were not received until 6/2/21, but they were reviewed in hopes of not continuing the hearing. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 B. and C.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANET JEAN CHUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 J.] No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. Paragraph 26 of the petition and paragraph 4 of the prayer are incomplete and insufficient. Who will receive the tangible personal property? How much cash is to be distributed to each beneficiary/heir? The number of shares of stock must be set forth, not just a percentage. [CRC 7.651(b) and LRSF 14.36 I.] If a non-pro rata distribution is to be made in order to distribute the tangible personal property that cannot be divided among five heirs, the written consent by all heirs must be filed. [LRSF 14.36 I.6.ii.] (The executor was appointed on 7/9/19, when the will was admitted. Letters were issued to her on 7/23/19. The transfer of cash into an estate bank account is not a change in the form of asset. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).]) Courtesy copies of papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/9/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA QUIGLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A. and B., and 14.60 J.] The register of actions, however, indicates that a receipt has been filed by Jennifer Cavecche Quigley, although there was no order for any distribution and the sole heir-at-law is the decedent's brother, Thomas Quigley. The Court to determine if Ana Robledo should be appointed Administrator of this estate with limited IAEA authority and no bond, although it is not clear that the estate has any assets, if the real property located at 1571-1573 Shrader Street in San Francisco is confirmed as an asset of the trust in case PTR-20-303987. Additional issues: 1) Additional powers were granted to the Special Administrator on 3/22/21 (to file responses to the trust petition here and in the Solano County case), although there was no special administrator as of 3/22/21, as Ana Robledo's Letters of Special Administration expired on 12/15/20 and have never been re-issued. 2) No proof of service of the proper Notice of [Second Amended] Petition to Administer Estate to the attorney for Norma E. Banks has been filed yet. Attorney James A. DuCharme filed a request for special notice on 12/21/20. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD BRENDEN DOOHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: It appears in the holographic will that the decedentâs name was Gerald Brendan (not Brenden) Dooher. If the name was misspelled on the pleadings, an amended petition will need to be filed and notice of the amended petition must be mailed and published. [CRC 7.53(a).] Item 5a(2)(a) or (b) of the petition must be answered. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedentâs parents and when did they die? The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete (no telephone number). The Proof of Holographic Instrument is incomplete (see item 4). Additionally, where the petitioner is also the sole beneficiary, a Proof of Holographic Instrument is more persuasive if signed by a disinterested party. [LRSF 14.18 A.] Courtesy copies of papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/2/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEWIS E. AND MARGARET E. BELL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/6/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/21/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
